title: Symphyotrichum boreale
intro: Symphyotrichum boreale, also known as northern bog aster, is a perennial plant species native to the boreal regions of North America, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and grass-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the severity of respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Symphyotrichum boreale include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and salves, to support its various health benefits.
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum boreale belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Symphyotrichum. This genus is a part of the tribe Astereae. Within the kingdom Plantae, Symphyotrichum boreale is classified as a dicotyledonous plant. It is also known as the northern fleabane or northern aster.
